>_ mend3 PRINCIPAL / STAFF ENGINEER · PLATFORM ARCHITECT

I design platforms that reduce organizational complexity.

The code matters — but the real goal is making systems, and the teams behind them, easier to evolve. Fifteen-plus years on the platforms other products are built on: multi-tenant SaaS, data platforms, integrations, and lately AI.

View Projects Book a call

Platform ArchitectureDistributed SystemsMulti-tenant SaaSAI InfrastructureDeveloper Experience

The engineer behind the platforms — career, focus and what I'm open to.

Curiosity & Focus

Architecture
in practice

Designing systems that scale, evolve and deliver value.

SaaS Ecosystem

AuthBillingTenantAPIEventsAnalytics

Most SaaS products end up rebuilding the same things — auth, billing, permissions, integrations. I'd rather treat those as platform capabilities than re-solve them in every product.

Multi-tenantBillingRBAC

Data Platform

Turning disconnected operational data into something teams can actually trust. Usually that's event streams, ETL and lakehouses — plus a lot of unglamorous consistency work.

StreamingLakehouseETL

AI Systems

Most AI projects stall because context lives in too many places. The interesting part is the infrastructure that lets a model reason over products, users, documents and operational data at once.

RAGAgentsMCP

Platform Engineering

A platform that needs tribal knowledge to run isn't finished. The good ones remove operational decisions instead of documenting them.

KubernetesCI/CDObservability

How I think

The hard part is rarely the code. It's keeping systems — and the teams behind them — easy to change as they grow.

Trade-offs I optimize for
Clarity over cleverness
Evolution over peak performance
Platform over product
Automation over process
Data over opinions
Architecture is mostly about making future changes cheaper.
Every repeated implementation is usually a missing platform capability.
Platforms should remove decisions, not create new ones.
I design systems that stay understandable as they grow.
Software scales when teams can evolve independently.
I spend more time removing complexity than adding features.

What I avoidaccidental complexity · unnecessary coupling · framework-driven architecture · lock-in · recurring manual work

Open Source

Building in public.

Latest Writing

Thoughts on architecture, engineering and AI.

Read on Medium
May 26, 2022 · Dev Genius
Going TypeStack with NX Monorepo and Docker (Part 1)
A long, hands-on tutorial — wiring a full project (APIs, app, Storybook, databases) on the Nx + Docker monorepo architecture.
1770Medium
May 29, 2022 · Dev Genius
Going TypeStack with NX Monorepo and Docker (Part 2)
Part 2 — extending the Nx workspace with Storybook, React and Tailwind CSS for more flexibility.
888Medium

Now

What I'm focused on.

Building DevShell Tech — making one foundation carry many products, so each new one costs less than the last
Proving a vertical can ship as a module, not a rewrite — finance/Open Finance and connectors riding the same core
Making local-first AI genuinely useful — Ella runs RAG, agents and memory on infra a company can own
Turning scattered docs and signals into a knowledge base the system queries — and keeps current on its own
Off-hours: local LLMs, edge AI and a Home Assistant setup that's a little too automated
